objective: Structured root-cause analysis through a seven-phase investigation protocol. Diagnoses why something broke, changed, or whether a claim is true. Produces a final report with root cause, evidence, hypotheses tested, and next steps. Does NOT implement fixes.

decision_points.decision: How much context already exists in conversation
branches: Mode clear from user language (broke/error/changed/verify) → set MODE, skip Phase 1
Topic named → set TOPIC, skip topic question
Evidence already shared → carry into Phase 2
Nothing clear → full sequence
User says broke/broken/error → MODE = symptom
User says changed/used to work/different → MODE = drift
User says verify/does X work/is it true → MODE = claim
Yes → derive slug from user input
No → ask 'What are we investigating?'
After 10 files + 10 commands without 2 strong hypotheses → pause, ask user to refine scope
Evidence sufficient → continue to hypothesis formation

decision_points.decision: Does prior decision/lesson directly explain symptom?
branches: Yes → flag immediately, investigation may be answered
No → continue to hypothesis formation
debate.py available → run review panel on strongest hypotheses
debate.py unavailable → skip, single-model assessment only

decision_points.decision: How confident are we in each hypothesis?
branches: Confirmed — evidence + panel agree, no contradictions
Likely — evidence supports, panel mostly agrees, minor gaps
Possible — some supporting evidence, significant gaps
Refuted — evidence contradicts or panel rejects
Yes, tracker available → persist findings with verdict/severity
Yes, tracker unavailable → document in report only
No MATERIAL findings → skip tracking
A) Fix it — proceed to /plan
B) Investigate further — dig deeper
C) Done — report is sufficient

ask_nodes.question: What are we investigating? Describe the symptom, drift, or claim in one sentence.
Ask user to refine investigation scope
ask_nodes.condition: After 10 files + 10 commands without narrowing to 2 hypotheses

ask_nodes.question: A) Fix it, B) Investigate further, C) Done
ask_nodes.condition: Always asked at end of investigation

success_criteria: Evidence gathered before hypothesis formation (evidence-first discipline)
Minimum 3 competing hypotheses formed from evidence
All hypotheses cite specific evidence sources (file:line, command output, git ref)
Cross-model panel run on strongest hypotheses if available
Final report written with all required sections
User presented with clear next action
failure_criteria: Investigation attempts to fix the problem
Intermediate progress or internal mechanics exposed to user
Scratch files not cleaned up after completion
